questions.question: Draw the truth table for a 4-to-2 encoder and derive the Boolean expressions for the outputs Y0 and Y1.
keyPoints: Show inputs D0-D3 with single HIGH condition
Output Y1Y0 changes: 00 for D0, 01 for D1, 10 for D2, 11 for D3
Y1 = D2 + D3 (MSB is HIGH for inputs 2,3
Y0 = D1 + D3 (LSB is HIGH for inputs 1,3

questions.question: Explain why a binary encoder cannot handle multiple simultaneous inputs and how this problem is overcome.
keyPoints: Binary encoder produces incorrect output with multiple 1s
Problem: ambiguity in output code
Solution: priority encoder assigns priority to inputs
Highest priority input determines output
Valid (V) output indicates if any input is active

questions.question: What is a priority encoder? Explain its need with a suitable example.
keyPoints: Priority encoder handles multiple active inputs
Assigns priority based on input position
Higher-order input gets priority if multiple inputs active
Used in keyboards where multiple keys might be pressed
Example: If I2 and I5 both HIGH, output corresponds to I5 (higher priority)

questions.question: Design a 2-to-1 priority encoder with inputs I0, I1 and outputs Y0 and V (valid output).
keyPoints: If I1=1, output Y0=1, V=1
If I1=0 and I0=1, output Y0=0, V=1
If both inputs 0, output Y0=0, V=0
Y1 = I1 (for 2-to-1, single output bit needed
V = I0 + I1 indicates valid input

questions.question: Explain the working of an octal-to-binary (8-to-3) encoder with a truth table.
keyPoints: 8 inputs represent octal digits 0-7
3 binary outputs represent the octal digit in binary
Each output is OR of specific inputs: Y0 = D1+D3+D5+D7, Y1 = D2+D3+D6+D7, Y2 = D4+D5+D6+D7
Output corresponds to decimal value of active input index

questions.question: List two practical applications of encoders in digital systems.
keyPoints: Keyboard encoding: Convert key presses to scan codes
Interrupt handling: Priority encoder identifies highest priority interrupt
Memory addressing: Select one memory location from many
Position encoding: Convert rotary/linear position to digital code

questions.question: Design a 4-to-2 priority encoder with inputs D0, D1, D2, D3 (D3 has highest priority) and outputs Y1, Y0, V. Derive the truth table and simplify Boolean expressions using K-maps.
keyPoints: Priority order: D3 > D2 > D1 > D0
Truth table: Show all 16 input combinations with correct outputs
For V=1 when any input is HIGH
Y1 = D2 + D3 (simplified from K-map)
Y0 = D3 + D1.D2' (D3 has priority or D1 when D2 is 0)
K-map simplification gives minimized expressions
Draw circuit using AND, OR, NOT gates

questions.question: Implement a decimal-to-BCD encoder using basic logic gates. Explain the working with truth table.
keyPoints: 10 inputs (0-9 decimal keys)
4 outputs for BCD code (0000 to 1001)
Each BCD bit is 1 for specific decimal inputs
OR gates combine appropriate inputs for each output
Explain truth table showing 10 input conditions

questions.question: Compare encoder and decoder. Design a simple system showing how an encoder and decoder can be used together in communication.
keyPoints: Encoder: 2^n inputs → n outputs (many to few)
Decoder: n inputs → 2^n outputs (few to many)
Inverse operations of each other
Combined system can transmit multiple signals over fewer wires
Example: 4-to-2 encoder at sender, 2-to-4 decoder at receiver
Saves wiring in multiplexed communication systems

questions.question: Design an 8-to-3 priority encoder with input priority I7 > I6 > I5 > I4 > I3 > I2 > I1 > I0. Provide the truth table and derive output expressions.
keyPoints: 8 inputs with I7 highest priority
3 binary outputs for 0-7
Valid output V indicates any active input
Priority logic: Higher index input dominates
Y2 = I4+I5+I6+I7 (MSB)
Y1 = I2+I3+I6+I7 + I4+I5 (excluding cases where higher priority active)
Y0 = I1+I3+I5+I7 + I4+I6 (excluding cases where higher priority active)
Complex priority logic requires careful K-map simplification
